summary: A validator-friendly lane for moving a dynamic object into a target region by contact force.
constraints: Place agent -> object -> region roughly collinear; alignment angle <= 35 degrees.
Place the object between the agent and region.
Keep object-to-region start distance <= 140 px, preferably 80-120 px.
Keep agent-to-object start distance <= 220 px, preferably 40-80 px for box/plate tasks so contact occurs quickly.
Keep the object passively stable before contact.
Add a real floor/shelf under the movable object; rails, gates, or blockers must not be the only support.
Use guide rails or corridor walls when the object can roll sideways.
Do not place solid blockers between object and region.
For pressure plates, use a non-blocking sensor target and place any gate after the plate, never between agent/object/plate.
repair_guidance: If the object moves away from the region, stage the agent directly behind the object and add short guide rails.
If sideways drift appears, narrow and straighten the push lane.
If forward velocity is low, reduce object friction/mass or increase agent_strength.
